text.title: Geek-Tier Software Architect Skill Index — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `architect-skill-index-geek-tier` must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: The methodology fires on a named trigger (event, threshold, schedule). Vague triggers ('when needed') are rejected.

rationale.source: Operating-system / cron design patterns; trigger-action behavioural research

rule.rationale: Methodologies without explicit triggers either fire constantly or never. A named trigger makes the cadence enforceable and lets reviewers audit whether the trigger actually fired.

rule.statement: Output shape is fixed by `content/02-output-contract.xml` (JSON Schema). Free-form output is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Methodologies produce reusable assets only when their output is shaped. Free-form output is one-off and dies on completion.

rule.statement: Every conclusion ties to evidence (data link, ticket, transcript, commit hash). Unevidenced conclusions are demoted to hypotheses.
rule.rationale: Without evidence, the artefact decays into folklore the moment the author rotates off. Evidence links are the cheapest mechanism that keeps the artefact reviewable months later.

rule.statement: Every artefact instance has a named owner (`role:handle`) accountable for the next-step actions. Outputs without owners are tagged stale at the next cadence.
rule.rationale: Anonymous outputs do not produce change. The owner is the cheapest mechanism that connects artefact to behaviour and reviewable consequences.

rule.statement: The methodology is reviewed against outcomes at a published cadence (monthly or quarterly). Methodologies that produce no measurable change are deprecated.
rule.rationale: Methodologies without an outcome review become folklore. The review loop is what keeps them honest and what gives the team the right to deprecate them when they stop earning their keep.
